source_pr.title: executor: fix correlated column range in table reader for the clustered index table
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #23076 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
We didn't handle the common handle for correlated column range in table reader for the clustered index table.

### What is changed and how it works?

When we build the range for the correlated column, use the other method to build it.

### Related changes



### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects



### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- `No release note

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select (select t2.c_str from t2 where t2.c_str = t1.c_str and t2.c_int = 10 order by t2.c_str limit 1) x from t1;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "goofy mestorf")) (ddl/db_integration_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select (select t2.c_str from t2 where t2.c_str = t1.c_str and t2.c_int = 10 order by t2.c_str limit 1) x from t1;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "goofy mestorf")) (ddl/db_integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select (select t2.c_str from t2 where t2.c_str = t1.c_str and t2.c_int = 10 order by t2.c_str limit 1) x from t1;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "goofy mestorf")) (ddl/db_integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select (select t2.c_str from t2 where t2.c_str = t1.c_str and t2.c_int = 10 order by t2.c_str limit 1) x from t1;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "goofy mestorf")) (ddl/db_integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select (select t2.c_str from t2 where t2.c_str = t1.c_str and t2.c_int = 10 order by t2.c_str limit 1) x from t1;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "goofy mestorf")) (ddl/db_integration_test.go)
